title: Decode vs prefill worker-count scaling on a 32-core x86_64 desktop

machine.role: agent-host (desktop / node 'desktop' in HARDWARE-CATALOG, run-on-bench-nodes-by-default EXCLUDED)
machine.contention: lightly contended: this is the live agent-host. ABSOLUTE tok/s here is NOT comparable to the uncontended Apple M3 Pro authority rows. The load-bearing claim is the WITHIN-RUN worker-count RATIO, which is robust to contention (governance: within-run ratios authoritative).

engine: fak-in-kernel Q8_0 (pure-Go, quantized weights+activations, int8xint8->int32 dot)
harness: cmd/modelbench -gguf <Q8 gguf> -lean, FAK_WORKERS pinned per row
finding: decode (single-token GEMV, memory/overhead-bound) and prefill (batched GEMM, compute-bound) have OPPOSITE worker-count optima. The single global FAK_WORKERS / GOMAXPROCS default (all 32 cores) lands in decode's WORST regime on small models. The decode over-threading penalty is largest on small models and shrinks as the model grows (2.5x at 1.5B -> 1.14x at 7B) -- the CPU-threading analogue of the repo's documented GPU launch-bound small-model artifact.
honest_fences: Within-run worker RATIO is the claim; absolute tok/s is contended-agent-host and NOT comparable to the idle M3 Pro authority rows (canonical M3 Pro 1.5B Q8 decode = 38.1 tok/s).

The decode decline is intrinsic, not pure contention: it already appears at 16w/24w (1.5B: 18.1->16.7->14.7), both of which leave cores free; the 32w cliff is partly the many-thread barrier cost and partly contention amplification on a fully-subscribed box.

Single global worker count cannot optimize decode and prefill simultaneously; phase-aware (or model-size-aware) worker selection would recover up to ~2.5x decode on small models with no cost to prefill or to large-model decode.

internal/model already optimized the decode worker pool (parallel.go spin-then-park barrier) and ships internal/model/bench_scaling.sh to measure exactly this tension; this artifact is the x86_64 32-core data point for that tool's question.
reproduce: for W in 4 8 16 24 32; do FAK_WORKERS=$W go run ./cmd/modelbench -gguf <Qwen2.5-1.5B-Q8.gguf> -lean -decode-steps 32 -decode-reps 5; done
